issues.description: Government control or restriction of advanced AI capabilities through trade, export, or compute access.

issues.display_name: Military and National Security Uses of AI
issues.description: Positions on the use of AI in military, intelligence, or national security contexts.
A candidate's general stance on regulating AI systems.
Positions on AI systems designed for emotional, relational, or social interaction.
Views on protecting minors from harms related to AI or AI-mediated platforms.
Positions on the development, regulation, or public impact of data center infrastructure as it relates to AI, including energy consumption and grid reliability concerns tied to AI workloads.
Positions on AI-driven job displacement, reskilling programs, unemployment policy, and tax policy for distributing AI-driven wealth.
Views on AI-generated misinformation, scam prevention, fraud targeting vulnerable populations, and synthetic media disclosure requirements.
Stance on federal preemption of state AI laws, states' rights to regulate AI, and the patchwork of local vs. national regulatory approaches.
Positions on AI training data rights, copyright protections for creators and publishers, and liability for AI-generated content.

positions.summary: Emphasizes importance of public comment before new data center construction begins

House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence chair; joined bipartisan effort to stop AI chip smuggling to China; stated 'the global AI race against China is moving much faster than many think' and 'Export controls on semiconductor manufacturing equipment and AI chips have strengthened America’s advantage in the AI Arms Race'
Sponsored FY26 Intelligence Authorization Act which encourages development of AI for US intelligence community
Voiced national security concerns on deepfakes manipulating comments from world leaders
Talks about the importance of testing and regulating the power of AI on The Contrarian; lectured on the topic "AI: To Innovate or Annihilate"
Described data centers as an important component of a booming 'AI economy' to the Arkansas Democrat Gazette; also talked about data centers causing an increase in utilities and water
Showed concern on how AI could rapidly replace white-collar jobs in an Arkansas Democrat Gazette article

2020 House Budget Committee hearing: stated 'Congress must ensure actions do not stifle innovation' and 'create regulatory environment that supports - not hinders - private industry' in AI development
Libertarian candidate promised to 'champion technological innovation and progress' and 'advocate for policies that harness power of AI and emerging technologies' while ensuring 'privacy protections'
Lists regulating AI by banning harmful deepfakes as a public policy area that Hocevar is passionate about
Emphasizes importance of public comment before new data center construction begins
Describes the superintelligence race between US and China as a Cold War and deems it a national security issue

Pro-innovation because of the US-China race, but has said that "maybe humankind would've been better off if we didn't discover this"; supports permitting reform and infrastructure acceleration rather than AI-specific regulation
Sponsored the SPEED Act to accelerate energy permitting for AI infrastructure; says data centers are "needed for AI" and "a necessary part of our future national security plans"
Supports Kids Internet and Digital Safety (KIDS) Act to protect children from online harms

Says growing presence of data centers and AI isn't a bad thing; wants developers to be transparent about plans; speculates that Cotton's DATA Act will lower energy costs; wants state-level regulators to have authority over data centers

Introduced the Chip Security Act, which would require location verification mechanisms on AI chips subject to export controls and mandate reporting to Commerce's Bureau of Industry and Security if chips are diverted or tampered with; also co-sponsored the America First GAIN AI Act to prioritize American companies' access to AI chips ahead of adversaries.
Introduced legislation that would make it easier for regulators to block large tech companies from buying up rivals or nascent competitors
Introduced DATA Act allowing AI data centers to bypass federal electricity regulations and build independent energy infrastructure; pushed off-grid power plan for AI data centers
Wrote a social media post about holding AI chatbot companies accountable
Cosponsored GUARD Act (S.3062) - Guidelines for User Age-verification and Responsible Dialogue Act of 2025
Voted to strike the section relating to AI preemption
Co-sponsored Kids Online Safety Act of 2023 (S.1409)
Voted to strike the section relating to AI preemption
Supports innovation but only with strong guardrails
Asserts that Congress needs to pass legislation that protects the public, especially kids
Says Congress should prevent AI from becoming a tool to undercut wages or replace workers without accountability
Argues that Congress should pass enforceable rules for AI generated deepfakes
Established task force that ensures effective coordination in addressing evolving AI challenges
Announces Dept. of Justice grant supporting Mississippi Internet Crimes Against Children Task Force
Involved in upskilling Mississippi, particularly rural Mississippi
Views AI as a tool for innovation but a tool that must be managed with a human-centric approach
Asserts that there should be federal safeguards to ensure AI doesn't lead to mass displacement of workers
Introduced bill that would require DHS to provide notification to Congress to explain all uses or extensions of transaction authority involving AI.

Discussed the need for Congress to secure the "virtual border" at a joint hearing between the Subcommittees on Border Security and Enforcement and Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Protection, which focused on the risks of digital technologies.
Says that AI is a helpful aid to law enforcement, but should not be a substitute for trained personnel.
Argues that if industry is overregulated, adversaries will advance AI capabilities before the US
Says that AI innovation must benefit everyone, not just tech industry giants
Former Lt. Governor supports workforce development and training for AI; advocates pro-worker solutions ensuring workers have a seat at the table in determining how AI is implemented in workplaces
Says that the Government should set clear guardrails and strong standards against deepfake fraud, especially in high risk contexts
Believes in improving export controls on sensitive technologies so China can't buy or steal criticial capabilities
Says Congress should prevent AI from being used for fraud and surveillance abuse.
